record.field: Langflow is a visual low-code builder for designing, testing, and deploying AI agents and RAG applications.

Langflow is a visual low-code builder for designing, testing, and deploying AI agents and RAG applications.

It pairs a drag-and-drop canvas with full Python extensibility, letting you wire together LLMs, vector stores, data sources, and tools into reusable flows. It is model-agnostic, working with providers such as OpenAI, Anthropic, Hugging Face, and local models via Ollama, and any workflow can be exported as JSON or published as an API or MCP server. Because flows run user-authored Python code, it is best operated behind authentication and kept current.

Key features
  • Visual drag-and-drop editor for multi-agent and RAG workflows
  • Model-agnostic: connect any LLM, embedding model, vector database, or API
  • Interactive playground with step-by-step execution and conversation management
  • Publish flows as REST APIs or MCP servers, or export them as portable JSON
  • Python customization of components and direct source-code access
  • Observability integrations (LangSmith, LangFuse) and self-hosted deployment
Best for

Developers and teams that want to prototype, iterate on, and ship LLM-powered agents and retrieval pipelines visually, while retaining the option to drop into Python and self-host the result.

field.name: tag_ids
field.eval: [Command.set([ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_self_hosted'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_ai'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_automation'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_workflow'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_no_code'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_dev_tools'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_llm')])]

record.field: services: langflow: image: langflowai/langflow:v1.1.1 ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:7860' depends_on: - postgres-langflow environment: - LANGFLOW_DATABASE_URL=postgresql://{{ db_username }}:{{ db_password }}@postgres-langflow:5432/langflow volumes: - ./langflow_data:/app/langflow container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network postgres-langflow: image: postgres:16 environment: POSTGRES_USER: {{ db_username }} POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ db_password }} POSTGRES_DB: langflow ports: - 5432 volumes: - ./langflow_postgres:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres_langflow' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres-langflow sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres-langflow for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres-langflow sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres-langflow sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres-langflow sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
